articles.title: Nagarjuna marks 40 years in cinema, cherishes May 23 memories
articles.description: Superstar Nagarjuna Akkineni cherishes May 23, the release date of his debut film 'Vikram' and his father ANR's last film 'Manam'. He marked 40 years in cinema with a heartfelt note, with son Naga Chaitanya also paying tribute.

articles.title: Vashu Bhagnani rejects claims song row with Taurani is 'sorted'
articles.description: Producer Vashu Bhagnani has rejected claims by Ramesh Taurani that the song controversy for 'Hai Jawani Toh Ishq Hona Hai' is 'sorted.' Bhagnani said the matter is in court and no settlement talks have taken place, calling it contempt of court.

articles.title: Rekha ended ties with Jeetendra after ‘timepass’ remark
articles.description: Allegations have surfaced that Rekha ended her rumored affair with Jeetendra upon hearing him nonchalantly label her 'timepass' while on the set of 'Bechara.' This poignant moment, chronicled in Yasir Usman's biography, reveals the significant emotional weight that such statements carried for the beloved actress.

articles.description: Rashmika Mandanna attended Anime Awards 2026 as a presenter alongside Winston Duke. The actress impressed in a custom Prabal Gurung gown while representing Indian cinema on the international stage.

articles.title: Actress Sukhi Bal summits a tough peak,eyes the Everest next
articles.description: Channeling her late father's adventurous spirit, actress Sukhi Bal has triumphantly scaled the formidable Mount Ama Dablam in Nepal, standing proud at 6,812 meters. This remarkable feat comes on the heels of her trek to Everest Base Camp and conquering Labouche Peak.

articles.title: Only now I am getting to show my versatility: Siddharth Nigam
articles.description: At just 25, Siddharth Nigam is breaking away from his boy-next-door persona, embracing a range of dynamic roles in films and OTT platforms. Starting his journey at the tender age of 10, he views this as the dawn of his career, drawing on the resilience instilled in him through gymnastics.

articles.title: Love quote of the day by Marilyn Monroe: “The real lover is a man who..."
articles.description: “The real lover is the man who can thrill you by kissing your forehead or smiling into your eyes or just staring into space.”— Marilyn MonroeMarilyn Monroe, the very image of glamour and flirtation, leaves us with this quietly profound notion of love. She doesn’t talk of grand gestures, expensive gifts, or dramatic declarations. No, she points to the small, everyday moments—the subtle, almost imperceptible acts that can make your heart skip. A kiss on the forehead, a smile across the room, even a shared silence where you both just are—these are the tiny sparks that for her define a “real lover.”In a world where romance is often measured by how much you spend, what you say, or how often you post about someone, Monroe’s words are a gentle reminder. Real love, she suggests, lives in the quiet, in the presence, and in the unspoken way two people can feel deeply connected without needing a script.

articles.title: Once did 13 shows at a time, now rejected at auditions: Anita Kanwal asks ‘why am I even living?’
articles.description: Anita Kanwal, who once worked on 13 shows simultaneously, opens up about audition rejections, self-doubt and questioning her purpose after 40 years in television.

Meanwhile, Serena Williams is growing her Serena Ventures and Serena's brand partnerships and Alexis Ohanian is growing his investment portfolio, Seven Seven Six. The extravagant lifestyle, business empire and status as a sports entrepreneur are still the center of attention for people throughout the globe.

articles.title: Quote of the day by Denzel Washington
articles.description: Denzel Washington, a true Hollywood standard, inspires aspiring artists with his powerful message on commitment and consistency. He emphasizes that success isn't easy, highlighting that struggle builds excellence and drives progress. Washington's legendary career and dedication to mentoring underscore his philosophy: "Without commitment, you will never start, but more importantly, without consistency, you will never finish."

articles.title: Are you a 'Peaky Blinders' fan? Here's a list of similar shows to add to your watchlist
articles.description: By order of ‘Peaky Blinders,’ Charlie Heaton was announced as the upcoming Shelby in an untitled spinoff series, with an unannounced release date for a bloodbath. However, a good crime series awaits no one. While the charm of the Shelbys brews, there exists a list of similar shows that promise to take the viewers on a political crime ride, while keeping them on the edge of their seats. From ‘Taboo’ to ‘Ozark,’ here’s a list of similar shows to watch until you can watch the sequel series of ‘Peaky Blinders.’

articles.title: Michael Jackson’s private chef wasn’t expecting the ‘Golden Temple’ reference in film - ‘We were excited’
articles.description: Michael Jackson's former private chef, Akasha Richmond, expressed excitement over her restaurant, The Golden Temple, being featured in the biopic 'Michael.' Richmond recalled the moment the restaurant was mentioned during a scene depicting Jackson's famous hair fire incident. She also praised the film for its portrayal of Jackson's creative process and performances.

articles.title: Thalapathy Vijay receives signed bat from Virat Kohli
articles.description: Actor Thalapathy Vijay received a special gift – a cricket bat signed by Indian cricketer Virat Kohli – during a meeting with MRF officials in Chennai. Photos of Vijay with the autographed bat quickly went viral, delighting fans and highlighting his known passion for cricket. This event also generated excitement ahead of his upcoming film, 'Jana Nayagan'.

articles.title: 'Feels like losing a child': Aamir Khan shares how film failures feel as Junaid Khan–Sai Pallavi's 'Ek Din' struggles at box office
articles.description: Aamir Khan revealed that film failures leave him emotionally devastated for months, saying he views every project like his own child. The actor said audience disappointment reflects a flaw in the filmmakers work and stressed the importance of accepting failures honestly. He also spoke about mourning setbacks, relying on genuine audience feedback and using test screenings to better understand whether a film is communicating the intended emotions to viewers.

articles.title: Aishwarya Rai’s ‘Poovukkul Oodum’ looks had royal roots
articles.description: Aishwarya Rai's mesmerizing performance in 'Poovukkul Oodum' from 'Jeans' leaves a lasting impression with its breathtaking costumes inspired by royal figures. Master designer Neeta Lulla skillfully crafted ensembles reminiscent of iconic queens like Cleopatra and Marie Antoinette, elevating the song to a pivotal moment in the annals of Indian cinema's fashion narrative, while also celebrating Aishwarya's timeless elegance.

articles.title: Navy SEAL Robert Harward’s face glitches on live tv? Terrifying 'fox news' interview goes mega viral
articles.description: Retired U.S. Vice Admiral Robert Harward has gone viral after a Fox News appearance sparked bizarre conspiracy theories online. While Harward was discussing Donald Trump’s Iran strategy, sanctions on Tehran and America’s military pressure campaign, social media users became fixated on something entirely different - his face. Several users on X claimed the former Navy SEAL looked “unnatural” during the interview and questioned whether he was wearing a realistic mask on live television. Some compared the viral clip to old internet conspiracy theories involving actor Jim Carrey, while others pointed to Harward’s ears and facial structure as “evidence.” The speculation spread rapidly across social media, overshadowing Harward’s actual comments on the U.S.-Iran conflict. During the Fox News segment, Harward argued that Trump held the upper hand against Iran through sanctions, economic pressure and a naval blockade. He claimed Tehran was being pushed toward “capitulation” and highlighted growing economic instability inside Iran.
